content.text:
Module:
Mobile Networks  |
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UEF31
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
1h30  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Discover wide area networks, including cellular networks covering all existing technologies
Introduction to the upcoming 6G generation
Analysis of WiMax urban wireless network performance and services
Coverage of LTE networks
Recommended Prerequisites
Good knowledge of basic networking, OSI reference model and TCP/IP protocols
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
2nd generation mobile networks
3rd generation mobile networks
4th generation mobile networks
5th generation mobile networks
Upcoming mobile networks: 6G
Assessment
Exam: 60%
Continuous assessment: 40%
Bibliographic References
From GSM to LTE-Advanced Pro and 5G: An Introduction to Mobile Networks and Mobile Broadband — Martin Sauter, Wiley and Sons Ltd, 2017.
Réseaux GSM — Xavier Lagrange, Philippe Godlewski, Sami Tabbane, Ed. Hermes, 2000.
WCDMA for UMTS: HSPA Evolution and LTE — Dr. Harri Holma, Dr. Antti Toskala, Ed. Wiley.
3G Wireless With WiMAX and Wi-Fi: 802.16 and 802.11 — Clint Smith et John Meyer, Ed. McGraw-Hill Professional.
L. Toutain, "Réseaux locaux et Internet", 3e édition, Hermes, 2003.
C. Huitema, "Le routage dans l'Internet", Eyrolles, 1995.
Jochen Schiller, Mobile Communications. Addison-Wesley, 2000.
Theodore S. Rappaport, Wireless Communications – Principles and Practice, Second Edition. Prentice Hall.
